content.text:
## Query LlmAgent declares sub_agents. ## Response ### Sources: Important: you can fetch the full content of any source using the fetch_url_content tool #### (google/adk-python/README.md)[https://raw.githubusercontent.com/google/adk-python/main/README.md] (Score: 0.62) - # Define individual agents greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...) task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...) # Create parent agent and assign children via sub_agents coordinator = LlmAgent( name="Coordinator", model="gemini-2.0-flash", description="I coordinate greetings and tasks.", sub_agents=[ # Assign sub_agents here greeter, task_executor ] ) ``` ### Development UI A built-in development UI to help you test, evaluate, debug, and showcase your agent(s). ### Evaluate Agents ```bash adk eval \ samples_for_testing/hello_world \ samples_for_testing/hello_world/hello_world_eval_set_001.evalset.json ``` ## 🤝 Contributing We welcome contributions from the community! Whether it's bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, or code contributions, please see our - [General contribution guideline and flow](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/contributing-guide/). - Then if you want to contribute code, please read [Code Contributing Guidelines](./CONTRIBUTING.md) to get started. ## 📄 License This project is licensed under the Apache 2.0 License - see the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details. --- *Happy Agent Building!* - ## ✨ Key Features - **Rich Tool Ecosystem**: Utilize pre-built tools, custom functions, OpenAPI specs, or integrate existing tools to give agents diverse capabilities, all for tight integration with the Google ecosystem. - **Code-First Development**: Define agent logic, tools, and orchestration directly in Python for ultimate flexibility, testability, and versioning. - **Modular Multi-Agent Systems**: Design scalable applications by composing multiple specialized agents into flexible hierarchies. - **Deploy Anywhere**: Easily containerize and deploy agents on Cloud Run or scale seamlessly with Vertex AI Agent Engine. ## 🤖 Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol and ADK Integration For remote agent-to-agent communication, ADK integrates with the [A2A protocol](https://github.com/google-a2a/A2A/). See this [example](https://github.com/google-a2a/a2a-samples/tree/main/samples/python/agents/google_adk) for how they can work together. ## 🚀 Installation ### Stable Release (Recommended) You can install the latest stable version of ADK using `pip`: ```bash pip install google-adk ``` The release cadence is weekly. - # Agent Development Kit (ADK) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE) [![Python Unit Tests](https://github.com/google/adk-python/actions/workflows/python-unit-tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/google/adk-python/actions/workflows/python-unit-tests.yml) [![r/agentdevelopmentkit](https://img.shields.io/badge/Reddit-r%2Fagentdevelopmentkit-FF4500?style=flat&logo=reddit&logoColor=white)](https://www.reddit.com/r/agentdevelopmentkit/) [![Ask DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/badge.svg)](https://deepwiki.com/google/adk-python)

An open-source, code-first Python toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying sophisticated AI agents with flexibility and control.

Important Links: Docs, Samples, Java ADK & ADK Web.

Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a flexible and modular framework for developing and deploying AI agents. While optimized for Gemini and the Google ecosystem, ADK is model-agnostic, deployment-agnostic, and is built for compatibility with other frameworks. ADK was designed to make agent development feel more like software development, to make it easier for developers to create, deploy, and orchestrate agentic architectures that range from simple tasks to complex workflows. --- ## ✨ Key Features - ## 🚀 Installation ### Stable Release (Recommended) You can install the latest stable version of ADK using `pip`: ```bash pip install google-adk ``` The release cadence is weekly. This version is recommended for most users as it represents the most recent official release. ### Development Version Bug fixes and new features are merged into the main branch on GitHub first. If you need access to changes that haven't been included in an official PyPI release yet, you can install directly from the main branch: ```bash pip install git+https://github.com/google/adk-python.git@main ``` Note: The development version is built directly from the latest code commits. While it includes the newest fixes and features, it may also contain experimental changes or bugs not present in the stable release. Use it primarily for testing upcoming changes or accessing critical fixes before they are officially released. ## 📚 Documentation Explore the full documentation for detailed guides on building, evaluating, and deploying agents: * **[Documentation](https://google.github.io/adk-docs)** ## 🏁 Feature Highlight ### Define a single agent: ```python from google.adk.agents import Agent from google.adk.tools import google_search root_agent = Agent( name="search_assistant", model="gemini-2.0-flash", # Or your preferred Gemini model instruction="You are a helpful assistant. Answer user questions using Google Search when needed.", description="An assistant that can search the web.", tools=[google_search] ) ``` ### Define a multi-agent system: Define a multi-agent system with coordinator agent, greeter agent, and task execution agent. Then ADK engine and the model will guide the agents works together to accomplish the task. ```python from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, BaseAgent # Define individual agents greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...) task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...) #### (google/adk-python/llms.txt)[https://raw.githubusercontent.com/google/adk-python/main/llms.txt] (Score: 0.61) - # Define individual agents greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...) task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...) # Create parent agent and assign children via sub_agents coordinator = LlmAgent( name="Coordinator", model="gemini-2.5-flash", description="I coordinate greetings and tasks.", sub_agents=[ # Assign sub_agents here greeter, task_executor ] ) Development UI A built-in development UI to help you test, evaluate, debug, and showcase your agent(s). Evaluate Agents adk eval \ samples_for_testing/hello_world \ samples_for_testing/hello_world/hello_world_eval_set_001.evalset.json 🤝 Contributing We welcome contributions from the community! Whether it's bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, or code contributions, please see our - General contribution guideline and flow . - Then if you want to contribute code, please read Code Contributing Guidelines to get started. 📄 License This project is licensed under the Apache 2.0 License - see the LICENSE file for details. Happy Agent Building! **Source:** [adk-python repository](https://github.com/google/adk-python) - # Agent Development Kit (ADK) Agent Development Kit (ADK) ## ADK Python Repository Agent Development Kit (ADK) An open-source, code-first Python toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying sophisticated AI agents with flexibility and control. Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a flexible and modular framework for developing and deploying AI agents. While optimized for Gemini and the Google ecosystem, ADK is model-agnostic, deployment-agnostic, and is built for compatibility with other frameworks. ADK was designed to make agent development feel more like software development, to make it easier for developers to create, deploy, and orchestrate agentic architectures that range from simple tasks to complex workflows. ✨ Key Features Rich Tool Ecosystem : Utilize pre-built tools, custom functions, OpenAPI specs, or integrate existing tools to give agents diverse capabilities, all for tight integration with the Google ecosystem. Code-First Development : Define agent logic, tools, and orchestration directly in Python for ultimate flexibility, testability, and versioning. Modular Multi-Agent Systems : Design scalable applications by composing multiple specialized agents into flexible hierarchies. Deploy Anywhere : Easily containerize and deploy agents on Cloud Run or scale seamlessly with Vertex AI Agent Engine. 🤖 Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol and ADK Integration For remote agent-to-agent communication, ADK integrates with the A2A protocol. See this example for how they can work together. 🚀 Installation Stable Release (Recommended) You can install the latest stable version of ADK using pip: pip install google-adk The release cadence is weekly. This version is recommended for most users as it represents the most recent official release. Development Version - Bug fixes and new features are merged into the main branch on GitHub first. If you need access to changes that haven't been included in an official PyPI release yet, you can install directly from the main branch: pip install git+https://github.com/google/adk-python.git@main Note: The development version is built directly from the latest code commits. While it includes the newest fixes and features, it may also contain experimental changes or bugs not present in the stable release. Use it primarily for testing upcoming changes or accessing critical fixes before they are officially released. 📚 Documentation Explore the full documentation for detailed guides on building, evaluating, and deploying agents: Documentation 🏁 Feature Highlight Define a single agent: from google.adk.agents import Agent from google.adk.tools import google_search root_agent = Agent( name="search_assistant", model="gemini-2.5-flash", # Or your preferred Gemini model instruction="You are a helpful assistant. Answer user questions using Google Search when needed.", description="An assistant that can search the web.", tools=[google_search] ) Define a multi-agent system: Define a multi-agent system with coordinator agent, greeter agent, and task execution agent. Then ADK engine and the model will guide the agents to work together to accomplish the task. from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, BaseAgent # Define individual agents greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...) task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...)

response.searchQuery:
In the context of artificial intelligence and agent-based systems, an `LlmAgent` declaring `sub_agents` suggests a hierarchical or modular architecture where an agent, in this case, an `LlmAgent`, is composed of or manages other agents, referred to as `sub_agents`. This design can be beneficial for several reasons:

1. **Modularity**: By breaking down the functionality of an `LlmAgent` into smaller, more manageable components (`sub_agents`), the system can become more modular. Each `sub_agent` can be designed to perform a specific task or set of tasks, making the overall system easier to understand, develop, and maintain.

2. **Scalability**: A system with `sub_agents` can be more scalable. As the complexity or the workload of the `LlmAgent` increases, additional `sub_agents` can be added to distribute the workload or to handle new functionalities without significantly altering the existing architecture.

3. **Flexibility**: The use of `sub_agents` allows for greater flexibility in system design. Different `sub_agents` can be implemented using different technologies, algorithms, or even programming languages, as long as they can communicate effectively with the `LlmAgent` and other components of the system.

4. **Specialization**: Each `sub_agent` can be specialized to perform a specific function better than a monolithic agent could. For example, in a natural language processing context, one `sub_agent` might specialize in language translation, while another focuses on text summarization.

5. **Fault Tolerance**: If one `sub_agent` fails or encounters an issue, the `LlmAgent` can potentially continue to operate, either by bypassing the faulty `sub_agent` or by duplicating its critical functions with another `sub_agent`.

6. **Learning and Adaptation**: In machine learning contexts, `sub_agents` can learn and adapt independently, allowing the overall system to evolve and improve over time in response to changing conditions or new data.

To declare `sub_agents` within an `LlmAgent`, the implementation details would depend on the programming language, framework, and specific requirements of the project. However, the general approach might involve:

- Defining a class or interface for `LlmAgent` that includes a method or property for managing `sub_agents`.
- Creating classes or instances for each type of `sub_agent`, each with its own set of methods and properties tailored to its specific function.
- Implementing communication or interface protocols that allow the `LlmAgent` to interact with its `sub_agents`, and vice versa.

Example (simplified and conceptual, in Python):
```python
class LlmAgent:
    def __init__(self):
        self.sub_agents = []

    def add_sub_agent(self, sub_agent):
        self.sub_agents.append(sub_agent)

    def execute_task(self, task):
        # Determine which sub_agent can handle the task
        for sub_agent in self.sub_agents:
            if sub_agent.can_handle(task):
                sub_agent.execute(task)
                break

class TranslationSubAgent:
    def can_handle(self, task):
        return task.type == "translation"

    def execute(self, task):
        # Perform translation
        pass

class SummarizationSubAgent:
    def can_handle(self, task):
        return task.type == "summarization"

    def execute(self, task):
        # Perform summarization
        pass

# Usage
llm_agent = LlmAgent()
translation_sub_agent = TranslationSubAgent()
summarization_sub_agent = SummarizationSubAgent()

llm_agent.add_sub_agent(translation_sub_agent)
llm_agent.add_sub_agent(summarization_sub_agent)

# Assuming a task object is defined elsewhere
task = Task("translation", "text to translate")
llm_agent.execute_task(task)
```
This example illustrates a basic structure where an `LlmAgent` manages a list of `sub_agents`, each capable of handling specific tasks. The actual implementation would require more detailed design and coding, depending on the project's specific needs and constraints.

parts.text:
The high-level wiki structure of the GitHub repository 'google/adk-python' is organized into the following main sections:

*   **1 Overview**
*   **2 Getting Started** (Installation, Quick Start Guide, Core Concepts)
*   **3 Agent System** (Agent Types and Hierarchy, LlmAgent Configuration, Agent Configuration Files, Agent Execution Lifecycle, Multi-Agent Orchestration, Callbacks and Hooks, Instructions and Prompts, Agent State and Context)
*   **4 Execution and Orchestration** (Runner, Invocation Context, Plugin System, LLM Flow Orchestration, App Configuration)
*   **5 Workflow Engine** (Workflow Concepts and Architecture, Node Types, Graph Definition and Routing, Parallel Processing and Fan-Out, Workflow HITL and Resumption, Dynamic Nodes and Advanced Patterns)
*   **6 LLM Integration** (Model Providers, BaseLlm Interface, Request and Response Processing, Streaming and Response Handling, Live Streaming and Real-time Interactions, Function Call Handling, Context Caching, Structured Output and Response Schemas, Provider-Specific Features)
*   **7 Tools and Extensions** (Tool Framework, Python Function Tools, MCP (Model Context Protocol) Tools, BigQuery Tools, OpenAPI and REST Tools, Built-in Tools, Tool Authentication, Tool Confirmation (HITL))
*   **8 Persistence and State** (Session Management, State Hierarchy, Event System and History, Artifact Storage, Memory Services, Database Session Service)
*   **9 User Interfaces** (Command Line Interface, Development Web UI, FastAPI Server, API Endpoints Reference, Service URIs and Configuration)
*   **10 Evaluation and Testing** (Evaluation Framework, Evaluation Sets and Cases, Evaluation Metrics and User Personas, Conformance Testing, Optimization)
*   **11 Deployment** (Deployment Overview, Cloud Run Deployment, Vertex AI Agent Engine Deployment, GKE Deployment, Deployment Configuration)
*   **12 Advanced Topics** (Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A), Session Resumability, Session Rewind, Event Compaction, Database Schema Management, Concurrency and Performance, Telemetry and Observability, BigQuery Analytics Plugin, Security and CORS Configuration, Code Execution, Environment Simulation, Integrations and Messaging, Event-Driven Triggers)
*   **13 Development and Contributing** (Local Development Setup, Project Structure, Code Quality and CI, Release Process, Contributing Guidelines)
*   **14 Glossary**

Regarding how an `LlmAgent` declares `sub_agents` in the ADK Python documentation, the system allows for the creation of multi-agent systems by defining individual `LlmAgent` instances and then assigning them as `sub_agents` to a "parent" `LlmAgent` (often a coordinator). This is demonstrated through the `sub_agents` parameter during the initialization of the parent `LlmAgent`.

For example, to declare `sub_agents`, you would first define your individual `LlmAgent` instances, such as `greeter` and `task_executor`:

```python
greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...)
task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.0-flash", ...)
```

Then, a `coordinator` `LlmAgent` can be created, and the previously defined agents are passed into its `sub_agents` list:

```python
coordinator = LlmAgent(
    name="Coordinator",
    model="gemini-2.0-flash",
    description="I coordinate greetings and tasks.",
    sub_agents=[  # Assign sub_agents here
        greeter,
        task_executor
    ]
)
```
This approach enables the coordinator agent to manage and orchestrate the tasks handled by its sub-agents, facilitating modular and hierarchical agentic architectures.

Summary: The 'ADK sub_agents' report outlines how a coordinator agent delegates tasks by identifying the most suitable sub-agent and transferring the query.
Details:
*   A coordinator agent, such as the `lead` agent, analyzes the user's request to determine the most appropriate sub-agent for handling the query.
*   Upon identifying the best-suited sub-agent, the coordinator utilizes a mechanism like `transfer_to_agent` to pass the entire context and control of the task to that specific agent.
*   This delegation ensures that specialized agents, like the researcher or reporter, handle tasks aligned with their expertise, optimizing workflow and response accuracy.

content.text:
# Agent Development Kit (ADK)

Agent Development Kit (ADK)

## ADK Python Repository

Agent Development Kit (ADK)

An open-source, code-first Python toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying sophisticated AI agents with flexibility and control.

Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a flexible and modular framework for developing and deploying AI agents. While optimized for Gemini and the Google ecosystem, ADK is model-agnostic, deployment-agnostic, and is built for compatibility with other frameworks. ADK was designed to make agent development feel more like software development, to make it easier for developers to create, deploy, and orchestrate agentic architectures that range from simple tasks to complex workflows.


✨ Key Features

Rich Tool Ecosystem
: Utilize pre-built tools, custom functions,
  OpenAPI specs, or integrate existing tools to give agents diverse
  capabilities, all for tight integration with the Google ecosystem.

Code-First Development
: Define agent logic, tools, and orchestration
  directly in Python for ultimate flexibility, testability, and versioning.

Modular Multi-Agent Systems
: Design scalable applications by composing
  multiple specialized agents into flexible hierarchies.

Deploy Anywhere
: Easily containerize and deploy agents on Cloud Run or
  scale seamlessly with Vertex AI Agent Engine.

🤖 Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol and ADK Integration

For remote agent-to-agent communication, ADK integrates with the A2A protocol. See this  example for how they can work together.


🚀 Installation


Stable Release (Recommended)


You can install the latest stable version of ADK using pip:


pip install google-adk



The release cadence is weekly.


This version is recommended for most users as it represents the most recent official release.


Development Version


Bug fixes and new features are merged into the main branch on GitHub first. If you need access to changes that haven't been included in an official PyPI release yet, you can install directly from the main branch:


pip install git+https://github.com/google/adk-python.git@main



Note: The development version is built directly from the latest code commits. While it includes the newest fixes and features, it may also contain experimental changes or bugs not present in the stable release. Use it primarily for testing upcoming changes or accessing critical fixes before they are officially released.


📚 Documentation


Explore the full documentation for detailed guides on building, evaluating, and
deploying agents:




Documentation




🏁 Feature Highlight


Define a single agent:


from google.adk.agents import Agent
from google.adk.tools import google_search

root_agent = Agent(
    name="search_assistant",
    model="gemini-2.5-flash", # Or your preferred Gemini model
    instruction="You are a helpful assistant. Answer user questions using Google Search when needed.",
    description="An assistant that can search the web.",
    tools=[google_search]
)



Define a multi-agent system:


Define a multi-agent system with coordinator agent, greeter agent, and task execution agent. Then ADK engine and the model will guide the agents to work together to accomplish the task.


from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, BaseAgent

# Define individual agents
greeter = LlmAgent(name="greeter", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...)
task_executor = LlmAgent(name="task_executor", model="gemini-2.5-flash", ...)

# Create parent agent and assign children via sub_agents
coordinator = LlmAgent(
    name="Coordinator",
    model="gemini-2.5-flash",
    description="I coordinate greetings and tasks.",
    sub_agents=[ # Assign sub_agents here
        greeter,
        task_executor
    ]
)



Development UI


A built-in development UI to help you test, evaluate, debug, and showcase your agent(s).




Evaluate Agents


adk eval \
    samples_for_testing/hello_world \
    samples_for_testing/hello_world/hello_world_eval_set_001.evalset.json



🤝 Contributing


We welcome contributions from the community! Whether it's bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, or code contributions, please see our
-
General contribution guideline and flow
.
- Then if you want to contribute code, please read
Code Contributing Guidelines
 to get started.

parts.text:
In ADK, a sub-agent is an individual agent assigned to a parent agent, enabling the construction of modular multi-agent systems where agents collaborate to achieve complex tasks.
